channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: The Download: AI health tools and the Pentagon’s Anthropic culture war
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?  In the last few months alone, Microsoft, Amazon, and OpenAI have all...

item.title: AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.
item.description: For decades, artificial intelligence has been evaluated through the question of whether machines outperform humans. From chess to advanced math, from coding to essay writing, the performance of AI models and applications is tested against that of individual humans completing tasks.  This...

item.title: Google Slashes Quantum Resource Requirements for Breaking Cryptocurrency Encryption
item.description: Google researchers have shown that breaking the encryption of Bitcoin and Ethereum requires 20x fewer qubits. The post Google Slashes Quantum Resource Requirements for Breaking Cryptocurrency Encryption appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
item.description: Chinese-speaking users are the target of an active campaign that uses typosquatted domains impersonating trusted software brands to deliver a previously undocumented remote access trojan named AtlasCross RAT. "The operation covers VPN clients, encrypted messengers, video conferencing tools,...

item.title: Exploitation of Critical Fortinet FortiClient EMS Flaw Begins
item.description: The SQL injection vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely, via crafted HTTP requests. The post Exploitation of Critical Fortinet FortiClient EMS Flaw Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is accelerating at an unprecedented rate. What is emerging is not simply a rise in the number of vulnerabilities or tools, but a dramatic increase in speed. Speed of attack, speed of exploitation, and speed of change across modern environments. This is the defining...

item.title: StrongSwan Flaw Allows Unauthenticated Attackers to Crash VPNs
item.description: Remotely exploitable, the integer underflow vulnerability impacts StrongSwan releases spanning 15 years. The post StrongSwan Flaw Allows Unauthenticated Attackers to Crash VPNs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Lloyds Data Security Incident Impacts 450,000 Individuals
item.description: A faulty software update led to the exposure of mobile banking users’ transactions to other users of the application. The post Lloyds Data Security Incident Impacts 450,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 31, 08:53 UTCInvestigating - Issue started at 1:33 PT / 8:53 UTC.

item.title: OPPO Schedules Find X9 Launch Event for April 21
item.description: OPPO announced its co-branded imaging launch event with Hasselblad will take place on April 21 in Chengdu, featuring the Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s Pro smartphones.

item.title: Zero Gravity Aircraft Raises $21M Pre-B Round
item.description: Zero Gravity Aircraft Industry, a Chinese eVTOL and eCTOL manufacturer, raised RMB 150 million (~$20.7M) in a Pre-B round, bringing its total funding over the past six months to RMB 800 million (~$110M).

item.title: Insilico Medicine Signs $2.75B Deal with Eli Lilly
item.description: Insilico Medicine (HKEX: 03696) entered a $2.75 billion global licensing deal with Eli Lilly for an AI-discovered oral GLP-1 drug, including a $115 million upfront payment.

item.title: Alibaba Launches Qwen3.5-Omni Multimodal Model
item.description: Alibaba's Qwen team launched Qwen3.5-Omni, a full-modal AI model supporting 113 languages for speech recognition, 36 for speech generation, and achieving 215 SOTA results across multimodal benchmarks.

item.title: Geek+ Reports $440M Revenue in 2025, Achieves Full-Year Profitability
item.description: Geekplus (2590.HK), the global leader in autonomous mobile robots, reported 2025 revenue of RMB 3.17 billion (~$437 million), up 31.6% YoY, with adjusted net profit of RMB 43.8 million (~$6 million).

item.title: Critical Vulnerability in OpenAI Codex Allowed GitHub Token Compromise
item.description: Researchers found an OpenAI Codex vulnerability that could have been exploited to compromise GitHub tokens. The post Critical Vulnerability in OpenAI Codex Allowed GitHub Token Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Axios Supply Chain Attack Pushes Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
item.description: The popular HTTP client known as Axios has suffered a supply chain attack after two newly published versions of the npm package introduced a malicious dependency. Versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 of Axios have been found to inject "plain-crypto-js" version 4.2.1 as a fake dependency. According to...

item.title: Bitboard version of Tetris AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The efficiency of game engines and policy optimization algorithms is crucial for training reinforcement learning (RL) agents in complex sequential decision-making tasks, such as Tetris. Existing Tetris implementations suffer from low simulation...

item.title: Multiverse: Language-Conditioned Multi-Game Level Blending via Shared Representation
item.description: arXiv:2603.26782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-level generation aims to translate natural language descriptions into structured game levels, enabling intuitive control over procedural content generation. While prior text-to-level generators are typically limited to a single game domain,...

item.title: Concerning Uncertainty -- A Systematic Survey of Uncertainty-Aware XAI
item.description: arXiv:2603.26838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper surveys uncertainty-aware explainable artificial intelligence (UAXAI), examining how uncertainty is incorporated into explanatory pipelines and how such methods are evaluated. Across the literature, three recurring approaches to uncertainty...

item.title: Neuro-Symbolic Learning for Predictive Process Monitoring via Two-Stage Logic Tensor Networks with Rule Pruning
item.description: arXiv:2603.26944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive modeling on sequential event data is critical for fraud detection and healthcare monitoring. Existing data-driven approaches learn correlations from historical data but fail to incorporate domain-specific sequential constraints and logical...

item.title: Compliance-Aware Predictive Process Monitoring: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.26948v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing approaches for predictive process monitoring are sub-symbolic, meaning that they learn correlations between descriptive features and a target feature fully based on data, e.g., predicting the surgical needs of a patient based on historical...

item.title: Mitigating Forgetting in Continual Learning with Selective Gradient Projection
item.description: arXiv:2603.26671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As neural networks are increasingly deployed in dynamic environments, they face the challenge of catastrophic forgetting, the tendency to overwrite previously learned knowledge when adapting to new tasks, resulting in severe performance degradation on...

item.title: Boundary-aware Prototype-driven Adversarial Alignment for Cross-Corpus EEG Emotion Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.26713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based emotion recognition suffers from severe performance degradation when models are transferred across heterogeneous datasets due to physiological variability, experimental paradigm differences, and device...

item.title: Learning to Select Visual In-Context Demonstrations
item.description: arXiv:2603.26775v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) adapt to visual tasks via in-context learning (ICL), which relies heavily on demonstration quality. The dominant demonstration selection strategy is unsupervised k-Nearest Neighbor (kNN) search. While simple,...

item.title: TED: Training-Free Experience Distillation for Multimodal Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.26778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge distillation is typically realized by transferring a teacher model's knowledge into a student's parameters through supervised or reinforcement-based optimization. While effective, such approaches require repeated parameter updates and...

item.title: A Step Toward Federated Pretraining of Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.26786v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid evolution of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) is bottlenecked by the saturation of high-quality public data, while vast amounts of diverse multimodal data remain inaccessible in privacy-sensitive silos. Federated Learning (FL) offers...

item.title: GeoBlock: Inferring Block Granularity from Dependency Geometry in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.26675v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Block diffusion enables efficient parallel refinement in diffusion language models, but its decoding behavior depends critically on block size. Existing block-sizing strategies rely on fixed rules or heuristic signals and do not account for the...

item.title: AlpsBench: An LLM Personalization Benchmark for Real-Dialogue Memorization and Preference Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.26680v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) evolve into lifelong AI assistants, LLM personalization has become a critical frontier. However, progress is currently bottlenecked by the absence of a gold-standard evaluation benchmark. Existing benchmarks either...

item.title: The Cognitive Divergence: AI Context Windows, Human Attention Decline, and the Delegation Feedback Loop
item.description: arXiv:2603.26707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper documents and theorises a self-reinforcing dynamic between two measurable trends: the exponential expansion of large language model (LLM) context windows and the secular contraction of human sustained-attention capacity. We term the...

item.title: Do Multilingual VLMs Reason Equally? A Cross-Lingual Visual Reasoning Audit for Indian Languages
item.description: arXiv:2603.26742v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-language models score well on mathematical, scientific, and spatial reasoning benchmarks, yet these evaluations are overwhelmingly English. I present the first cross-lingual visual reasoning audit for Indian languages. 980 questions from...

item.title: LogicDiff: Logic-Guided Denoising Improves Reasoning in Masked Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.26771v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion language models (MDLMs) generate text by iteratively unmasking tokens from a fully masked sequence, offering parallel generation and bidirectional context. However, their standard confidence-based unmasking strategy systematically...

item.title: [AINews] The Last 4 Jobs in Tech
item.description: a quiet day lets us examine an interesting mental model

item.title: Mistral: Voxtral TTS, Forge, Leanstral, & what's next for Mistral 4 — w/ Pavan Kumar Reddy & Guillaume Lample
item.description: Mistral is one of the world's leading frontier model labs, and has just launched Voxtral TTS, their latest step in their strategy to offer open frontier intelligence for every modality.

item.title: Increase in 5xx Errors in Fortaleza, Brazil datacenter (FOR)
item.description: Mar 30, 18:30 UTCResolved - Cloudflare identified an increase in 5xx errors with our CDN service in the Fortaleza, Brazil datacenter (FOR). Users may have experienced an increase in errors between 18:44 UTC and 19:12 UTC on March 30, 2026. The issue has been resolved

item.title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
item.description: A previously unknown vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated without user knowledge or consent, according to new findings from Check Point. "A single malicious prompt could turn an otherwise ordinary conversation into a covert exfiltration channel,...

item.title: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?
item.description: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A couple of days earlier, Amazon had announced that Health AI, an LLM-based tool previously restricted...

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Uses ClickFix and WMI Persistence to Steal Browser Credentials
item.description: A new campaign has leveraged the ClickFix social engineering tactic as a way to distribute a previously undocumented malware loader referred to as DeepLoad. "It likely uses AI-assisted obfuscation and process injection to evade static scanning, while credential theft starts immediately and captures...

item.title: The Pentagon’s culture war tactic against Anthropic has backfired
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Last Thursday, a California judge temporarily blocked the Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a supply chain risk and ordering government agencies to stop using its...

item.title: Evolutionary Discovery of Reinforcement Learning Algorithms via Large Language Models
item.description: Reinforcement learning algorithms are defined by their learning update rules, which are typically hand-designed and fixed. We present an evolutionary framework for discovering reinforcement learning algorithms by searching directly over executable update rules that implement complete training...

item.title: Elevated delays in Actions workflow runs and Pull Request status updates
item.description: Mar 30, 13:25 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 30, 13:25 UTCUpdate - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure...

item.title: Latest open artifacts (#20): New orgs! New types of models! With Nemotron Super, Sarvam, Cohere Transcribe, & others
item.description: New orgs! New types of models! With Nemotron Super, Sarvam, Cohere Transcribe, & others

item.title: The Download: brainless human clones and the first uterus kept alive outside a body
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones  After operating in secrecy for years, R3 Bio, a California-based...

item.title: Why cyber defenders need to be ready for frontier AI
item.description: Understanding the threats and staying ahead of the adversary

item.title: Vulnerability affecting F5 BIG-IP APM
item.description: The NCSC is encouraging UK organisations to mitigate an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability affecting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager.

item.title: Workers Assets increased upload failure
item.description: Mar 30, 11:51 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue impacting Workers Assets uploads. Customers may be seeing an increase in Workers Assets upload failures.This issues does not affect the serving of Workers Assets traffic. We are working to mitigate this problem. More...

item.title: Beyond Cosine Similarity: Zero-Initialized Residual Complex Projection for Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
item.description: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) is fundamentally challenged by representation entanglement, where aspect semantics and sentiment polarities are often conflated in real-valued embedding spaces. Furthermore, standard contrastive learning suffers from false-negative collisions, severely...

item.title: Synonymix: Unified Group Personas for Generative Simulations
item.description: Generative agent simulations operate at two scales: individual personas for character interaction, and population models for collective behavior analysis and intervention testing. We propose a third scale: meso-level simulation - interaction with group-level representations that retain grounding in...

item.title: Dogfight Search: A Swarm-Based Optimization Algorithm for Complex Engineering Optimization and Mountainous Terrain Path Planning
item.description: Dogfight is a tactical behavior of cooperation between fighters. Inspired by this, this paper proposes a novel metaphor-free metaheuristic algorithm called Dogfight Search (DoS). Unlike traditional algorithms, DoS draws algorithmic framework from the inspiration, but its search mechanism is...

item.title: Physically Inspired Gaussian Splatting for HDR Novel View Synthesis
item.description: High dynamic range novel view synthesis (HDR-NVS) reconstructs scenes with dynamic details by fusing multi-exposure low dynamic range (LDR) views, yet it struggles to capture ambient illumination-dependent appearance. Implicitly supervising HDR content by constraining tone-mapped results fails in...

item.title: Account creation and login are unstable across all surfaces
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational)
